root_text.sn45.173:1.5: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, catunnaṁ upādānānaṁ abhiññāya pariññāya parikkhayāya pahānāya …pe… ayaṁ ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo bhāvetabbo”ti.

translation_text.sn45.173:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these four kinds of grasping.
translation_text.sn45.173:1.3: Grasping at sensual pleasures, views, precepts and observances, and theories of a self.
translation_text.sn45.173:1.4: These are the four kinds of grasping.
translation_text.sn45.173:1.5: The noble eightfold path should be developed for the direct knowledge, complete understanding, finishing, and giving up of these four kinds of grasping.”
